title: Berlin Beer Gardens - Quick Summary
items: Berlin's beer gardens (Biergärten) typically open from **April/May to September/October** and are a local summer lifestyle ritual.
**Prater Biergarten** in Prenzlauer Berg is Berlin's oldest, operating under chestnut trees since **1837**.
For scenic views, **Café am Neuen See** sits on a lake in Tiergarten, while **Zenner** and **Zollpackhof** offer riverside spots on the Spree.
**BRLO Brwhouse** at Park am Gleisdreieck is a modern craft beer hub built from **38 recycled shipping containers**.
Most gardens are **self-service (Selbstbedienung)**; look for separate food and drink windows, pay a glass deposit (Pfand), and carry cash for small purchases.
Traditional etiquette allows you to **bring your own food** (but not drinks) to self-service tables, though buying local pretzels, sausages, and Obatzda is common.
